### How MoXi® replaced their locked-in LOS tool with Firma.dev, and set it up in two days.

Jun 11, 2026 [...] — Rasmus Rowbotham, Founder, FoundBase

## The Switch

Rasmus found Firma.dev and saw something different: transparent pricing on the website, pay-as-you-go at €0.029 per envelope, and no requirement to speak to anyone before getting started. He signed up, got an API key, and had a working integration built in a single evening.

The integration covers the full contract lifecycle inside FoundBase: templates, a collaborative document editor, the signature flow, and webhook-triggered automations that fire when contracts are signed (emails, notifications, deal creation). FoundBase's users never leave the platform to sign.

The ease of implementation and the pricing were the biggest factors. I could just start building.

— Rasmus Rowbotham, Founder, FoundBase

## The Result

results.title: Latest News - Apple Developer
results.content:
iOS 26, iPadOS 26, macOS Tahoe 26, tvOS 26, visionOS 26, and watchOS 26 will soon be available to customers worldwide — which means you can now submit apps and games that take advantage of Apple’s broadest design update ever.

Build your apps and games using the Xcode 26 Release Candidate and latest SDKs, test with TestFlight, and submit for review to the App Store. By taking advantage of the new design and Liquid Glass, the Foundation Models framework, the new Apple Games app, and more, you can deliver even more unique experiences on Apple platforms.

Starting April 2026, apps and games uploaded to App Store Connect need to meet the following minimum requirements. [...] Learn more about WWDC26

## Apple Developer is now on bilibili and LinkedIn

March 17, 2026

Check out Apple Developer on bilibili and LinkedIn to learn about the latest news, announcements, videos, and events — including the Worldwide Developers Conference and Meet with Apple activities — for the developer community.

## Adjustments to the China storefront of the App Store on iOS and iPadOS

March 12, 2026

Apple is making changes to the App Store in China following discussions with the Chinese regulator.

As of March 15, 2026, changes will be made to the commission rates that apply to the China mainland storefront of the App Store on iOS and iPadOS. [...] Image 14: Find out what’s new for Apple developers

## Get ready with the latest beta releases

March 30, 2026

The beta versions of iOS 26.5, iPadOS 26.5, macOS 26.5, tvOS 26.5, visionOS 26.5, and watchOS 26.5 are now available. Get your apps ready by confirming they work as expected on these releases. And make sure to build and test with Xcode 26.5 beta to take advantage of the advancements in the latest SDKs.

results.title: I/O 2026: Welcome to the agentic Gemini era - Google Blog
results.content:
## Gemini 3.5 Flash

Gemini 3 launched a few months ago, with a full family of models. It’s our most adopted series yet. We've loved seeing developers use Flash as their daily driver, and build incredible experiences with Pro's deep reasoning and multimodal capabilities. We’ve been hard at work on improving these models, especially focused on agentic coding, long-horizon tasks and real-world workflows.

Today, we’re introducing Gemini 3.5 Flash, our first in a series of models combining frontier intelligence with action. Two things I’d highlight: [...] # Google I/O 2026: Sundar Pichai’s opening keynote

results.title: Build 2026: Furthering Windows as the trusted platform for ...
results.content:
## Introducing Project Solara

We’re introducingProject Solara,a new platform built from the ground up to power agent-driven experiences, including two new concept devices that reimagine how this comes to life. With agents becoming a both a new unit of programming and an emerging new unit of human-to-machine interaction,the mission of Project Solara is to pioneer agent-first experiences that are shaped around you: your agents, your tasks, your environment, under your control.

## Developer-optimized Windows 11 experience to build and ship faster [...] Surface RTX Spark Dev Box delivers GPU-first AI performance with the new NVIDIA RTX Spark silicon, providing 1 petaflop of AI compute( and 128GB of unified memory shared dynamically across CPU and GPU in a single memory address space. This hardware foundation is designed for model optimization, fine-tuning and large inference workloads. By making these workloads practical to run locally, it reduces reliance on cloud only workflows, helping avoid recurring token costs and usage spikes while keeping iteration fast and predictable. [...] With Windows 11 PCs powered by capable silicon from AMD, Intel, NVIDIA and Qualcomm including workstation-class machines powered by AMD Ryzen™ AI MAX+ 395, new NVIDIA RTX Spark, and data-center-class systems like NVIDIA DGX Station for Windows, developers now have access to unmetered, tiered AI capabilities tailored to specific needs, from everyday development to frontier-class tasks.

## Announcing Surface RTX Spark Dev Box available later this year

results.title: Forbes 2026 AI 50 List | Top Artificial Intelligence Companies
results.content:
They’re not the only ones training models: San Francisco-based Physical Intelligence has raised $1 billion in funding to train foundational models for robots by collecting data from human teleoperators that control humanoids in realistic environments like kitchens and bedrooms. French startup Mistral is selling its open-weight models not just to large corporations like Cisco but also to European government agencies, for whom its local roots are a winning differentiator. (Read our feature on Mistral here.) Cambridge-based AI startup Suno is coming for an entire creative field with its music generation models. [...] Despite those consolidations, this year was among the most competitive for the AI 50 list. _Forbes_ received hundreds of applications. Applicants do not pay a fee to be considered and are judged for their business promise, technical talent and use of AI through a quantitative algorithm and qualitative judging panels. Companies are encouraged to share data on diversity, and our list aims to promote a more equitable startup ecosystem. This year, Forbes launched its first ever AI 50 Brink list to highlight 20 up-and-coming early stage AI startups. For more, see our full package of coverage, including a detailed explanation of the list methodology, videos and analyses on trends in AI.

  

 Reporters: Sofia Chierchio, Richard Nieva, Anna Tong 

 Deputy Editor: Elisabeth Brier [...] 1

1-50 of 50 

Inquire for List DataRequest license data and information spreadsheets from this Forbes Ranking for research, reference and other limited use options.
